name: Cloudflare
description: Cloudflare API v4 — DNS records, zones, registrar domains, and cache management. Authenticate with a scoped API token (Zone:Read + DNS:Edit for DNS, plus Domain:Edit for Registrar).

credential_fields.name: token
credential_fields.label: API Token
credential_fields.description: Cloudflare API token (create at dash.cloudflare.com/profile/api-tokens). Recommended template: 'Edit zone DNS' for DNS work; add 'Domain:Edit' permissions for Registrar tools.

credential_fields.name: account_id
credential_fields.label: Account ID
credential_fields.description: Cloudflare account ID (32-char hex string from the dashboard sidebar). The runner substitutes this into account-scoped paths ({{account_id}}) at execution time, so leaving it blank breaks every Tunnel and Registrar call.

tools.name: list_zones
tools.description: List Cloudflare zones (sites) accessible to the API token. Use this to find the zone_id needed for DNS-record and cache operations.

tools.name: get_zone
tools.description: Get a single zone's details — name servers, plan, status, paused state, owner, original registrar.

tools.name: list_dns_records
tools.description: List DNS records in a zone with optional filtering by type, name, content, and proxy status. Returns record IDs needed for update/delete.

name.description: Filter by record name (exact match, e.g. 'sub.example.com').

tools.name: get_dns_record
tools.description: Get a single DNS record by ID.

tools.name: create_dns_record
tools.description: Create a new DNS record. For MX records also supply 'priority'; for SRV records use the 'data' object instead of 'content' (target/port/priority/weight).

name.description: Record name (e.g. 'sub.example.com', or '@' / the apex name for root).

content.description: Record content (e.g. IPv4 address for A, hostname for CNAME, target for MX). Omit for record types that use 'data' instead.

data.description: Structured data for record types that need it (SRV, CAA, LOC, etc.). E.g. SRV: { service, proto, name, priority, weight, port, target }.

ttl.description: TTL in seconds (60–86400). Use 1 for automatic.

proxied.description: Proxy through Cloudflare (orange cloud). Only for A, AAAA, CNAME.

priority.description: Priority for MX/URI records (lower = higher priority).

tools.name: update_dns_record
tools.description: Partially update an existing DNS record (PATCH semantics — unspecified fields are preserved). Use this for routine edits. For full-replace semantics, batch_dns_records supports PUT operations.

tools.name: batch_dns_records
tools.description: Apply many DNS-record changes in a single transactional API call. Operations are executed in order: deletes → patches → puts → posts. Use for migrations, bulk renames, or any change set you want to roll back atomically on failure.

patches.description: Partial updates (each item must include 'id' plus the fields to change).

puts.description: Full overwrites (each item must include id + type + name + content; unspecified fields revert to defaults).

posts.description: New records to create (id not required; same shape as create_dns_record body).

tools.name: purge_cache
tools.description: Purge cached files from Cloudflare's edge for a zone. Pass purge_everything=true to flush the entire zone, or supply specific URLs / tags / hosts / prefixes to do a targeted purge.

purge_everything.description: Flush the entire zone cache. Cannot be combined with the targeted fields below.

items.description: Either a URL string or { url, headers } object for header-aware purging.

files.description: Specific URLs to purge (max 30 per call on Free/Pro).

tools.name: list_domains
tools.description: List domains registered with Cloudflare Registrar for the configured account. Returns names, expiration dates, auto-renew, transfer-lock and privacy status.

tools.name: get_domain
tools.description: Get registrar details for a specific domain — registration status, expiry, name servers, locked / privacy state, transfer status.

tools.name: update_domain
tools.description: Update registrar settings for a domain — auto-renew, WHOIS privacy, registrar lock, name servers. PUT semantics: include only the fields you want to change; unspecified fields are left as-is.

tools.name: list_tunnels
tools.description: List Cloudflare Tunnels (cfd_tunnel) in an account. Use this to find an existing tunnel by name before creating a duplicate. Filter is_deleted=false to skip soft-deleted tunnels.

tools.name: create_tunnel
tools.description: Create a Cloudflare Tunnel. Returns the tunnel id and a connector token (used by 'cloudflared tunnel run --token <token>'). Set config_src=cloudflare to manage ingress through the API (update_tunnel_configuration); use config_src=local if the connector reads ingress from a local config.yml instead.

name.description: Tunnel name. Must be unique within the account; surfaces in the dashboard and the runs log.

config_src.description: Where ingress config lives. 'cloudflare' = managed via this API; 'local' = managed via cloudflared's local config.yml.

tunnel_secret.description: Optional 32-byte base64 secret for legacy named-tunnel credential JSON. Omit for token-based connectors (the modern path).

tools.name: get_tunnel_token
tools.description: Fetch the connector token for an existing tunnel — needed when adopting a tunnel created earlier (the create_tunnel response only returns the token once). The token is what 'cloudflared tunnel run --token <token>' consumes.

tools.name: update_tunnel_configuration
tools.description: Replace the tunnel's ingress configuration. Body must include a 'config' object with an 'ingress' array; the last entry must be a catch-all (e.g. {"service": "http_status:404"}). This is a full replace, not a merge — include every rule you want to keep.

config.description: Ingress + warp-routing config. Shape: { ingress: [{hostname?, service, path?, originRequest?}, …], warp-routing?: {enabled: bool} }. Always end ingress with a catch-all {service: 'http_status:404'} rule.

tools.name: delete_tunnel
tools.description: Delete a Cloudflare Tunnel. The tunnel must have no active connections; stop the connector first. Soft-deleted by default — pass cascade=true to also delete dependent connections.
